projects.title: WeaveMind
projects.description: A novel pattern-driven beamforming architecture designed for 6G environments. It utilizes Local Context Memory and Stability Anchors to dramatically improve signal reliability and targeting in high-density areas.
projects.content:
### Introduction to WeaveMind

WeaveMind is an innovative, pattern-driven beamforming architecture engineered specifically for the highly complex environments of 6G wireless networks. By shifting away from traditional static or feedback-heavy dynamic beam selection, WeaveMind utilizes predictive machine learning algorithms to anticipate device movements and environmental shifts, aligning spatial targeting with surgical precision.

### Key Technical Architecture

1. **Local Context Memory (LCM):** Utilizes spatial temporal history of the surrounding radio environment to predict shadowing and interference patterns before they occur.
2. **Stability Anchors:** A set of signal stabilization algorithms that prevent packet drop during fast handoffs and dynamic channel state information (CSI) fluctuations.

### Outcomes & Results

- Improved signal reliability in high-mobility scenarios by up to **35%**.
- Reduced beam alignment latency from 12ms to **2.4ms**.
- Substantial throughput consistency across dense urban cells.

projects.title: AI-Driven Fuzzing
projects.description: A highly scalable framework for vulnerability analysis in 5G traffic steering algorithms. It employs NSGA-II multi-objective optimization to isolate hidden protocol weaknesses before they compromise network stability.
projects.content:
### Vulnerability Analysis in Traffic Steering

AI-Driven Fuzzing is a highly scalable security framework designed to isolate and mitigate adversarial weaknesses within 5G traffic steering controllers. By introducing automated state-based testing, the framework ensures next-generation routing policies are resilient against protocol-level attacks and edge-case exploits.

### Core Methodology

- **Multi-Objective Optimization:** Uses the NSGA-II genetic algorithm to explore the massive input-state space of traffic steering controllers, finding scenarios that simultaneously maximize routing conflict and packet loss.
- **Protocol Fuzzing:** Synthesizes irregular RAN control messaging packets, simulating adversarial edge environments to test fallback reliability.

projects.title: HARMONY
projects.description: HARMONY brings agentic AI to O-RAN testing: a multi-agent system that autonomously manages, diagnoses, and reconfigures a live 5G SA testbed through natural language.
projects.content:
### Agentic AI in Open RAN testing

HARMONY is a state-of-the-art multi-agent AI framework designed to orchestrate, diagnose, and self-heal 5G Standalone (SA) deployments under Open RAN (O-RAN) architectures. It acts as an autonomous operations controller, permitting network operators to communicate complex test sequences, slice definitions, and fault diagnostics through simple natural language.

### How It Works

- **Specialized AI Agents:** HARMONY splits core tasks across three distinct specialized agent types:
  1. **Diagnostics Agent:** Analyzes live metrics and log files from the central unit (CU) and distributed unit (DU) to find protocol faults.
  2. **Configuration Agent:** Updates radio configurations via standardized REST APIs and Netconf/YANG schemas.
  3. **Verification Agent:** Deploys virtual user equipment (UEs) to test throughput and packet latency, verifying configuration updates.
- **Autonomous Self-Healing Loop:** If a fault is diagnosed, the agents cooperatively plan a rollback or mitigation sequence without manual engineering intervention.

projects.title: FairShare
projects.description: A decentralized resource sharing protocol for edge devices. Utilizes federated learning to allow edge clusters to cooperatively manage power limits without central cloud orchestrators.
projects.content:
### Dynamic Edge Orchestration

FairShare is a decentralized, cooperative resource sharing protocol designed to enable edge compute nodes to optimize cluster power distribution and resource load-balancing autonomously. By avoiding centralized cloud orchestrators, FairShare eliminates single points of failure and significantly minimizes network overhead.

### Core Technologies

- **Federated Resource Allocation:** Nodes communicate model parameters regarding their power margins and compute usage rather than raw traffic data.
- **Adaptive Power Control:** Keeps total edge cluster power usage below set bounds while ensuring critical low-latency edge jobs receive optimal computing resource shares.
